methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Aepnus Technology is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Lukas Hackl and Bilen Akuzum that has raised $10.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) operating in the same sector, including later-stage companies such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value, applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $26M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median across these three peers is $150M, establishing a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $23M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $88M. The spread reflects meaningful uncertainty inherent in early-stage climate tech valuations: stage-progression methods are conservative proxies for pre-revenue or early-revenue businesses, while peer medians may not fully capture Aepnus's specific technology, market timing, or capital efficiency relative to the comp set. The most material uncertainty is whether Aepnus's technical or market positioning more closely resembles the lower or upper quartile of Series A peers.
